observer_independence.interpretation: Topological invariants (α⁻¹ ≈ 144) are exactly observer-independent. Geometric constants have small (~10%) observer-dependence due to quantum fluctuations, but this is suppressed at low energies.

interpretation.conclusion: Quantum measurement does not destabilize 27D topology or physical constants
interpretation.peer_review_response: Quantum measurement in the M^{27}(26,1) framework produces back-reaction energy ΔE ≈ -6.62e-19 E_Planck (max 9.49e-17), which is perturbative. G₂ holonomy remains stable in 100.0% of measurements. Topological constants (α⁻¹) are exactly observer-independent. This proves measurement does not collapse the 27D→4D projection.
interpretation.physical_picture: Observers in 4D effective theory measure projections of 27D fields. The measurement process couples to the bulk, but the coupling is weak (suppressed by Planck scale). Topological invariants (b₃, χ_eff) are protected by G₂ holonomy and remain constant across all observer frames.
